data.title: FWD Singapore and Ascend Asia partner on financial advisory services
data.description:
Firms will collaborate on analytics, technology, and advisory tools.

FWD Singapore and Ascend Asia Financial Services Group have signed a long-term strategic partnership to expand access to insurance and investment solutions through Ascend Asia’s open architecture financial advisory platform.

Under the partnership, Ascend Asia customers across retail, mass affluent, and high-net-worth segments will gain access to FWD’s financial advisory solutions, including life insurance, investment-linked products, and wealth offerings.

Ascend Asia Asset Management funds will also be added as sub-funds to FWD’s investment-linked product portfolio, giving customers more investment options.

The partnership combines FWD’s product capabilities and digital servicing with Ascend Asia’s adviser network, governance framework and technology platform.

The two companies will also collaborate on analytics and technology initiatives to improve customer insights and advisory tools.

FWD Singapore CEO Adrian Vincent said the partnership supports the growth of Singapore’s open architecture financial advisory model and aims to strengthen financial planning services.

“With consumers becoming increasingly discerning about how they access financial advice and ongoing support, we are confident in the future of the open architecture model and congratulate Ascend Asia on its investment in strengthening the independent financial advisory ecosystem,” he added.

Ascend Asia CEO Tomas Urbanec said the collaboration will provide advisers and clients with wider access to customer-focused solutions and technology-enabled experiences.

“Our mission is to empower our member firms with the strategic investment, structure and tools they need to thrive and support their clients along their wealth journeys,” he said. “Close collaboration with our partners in areas like product and customer proposition development extends choice for customers and amplifies the impact of our open architecture model.

The partnership was marked at the opening of Ascend Asia’s Wealth Centre, with executives from both companies and representatives from Ascend Asia’s member firms in attendance.

data.title: MTI overhaul elevates energy under new name, leadership
data.description:
DPM Gan Kim Yong and Minister Tan See Leng will co-lead the renamed ministry. 

The Ministry of Trade and Industry (MTI) is set for an overhaul, including a new name and leadership changes, reflecting an expanded focus on energy policy, according to Prime Minister Lawrence Wong.

The ministry will be renamed the Ministry of Energy, Trade and Industry (METI) from 1 October, he announced at the Press Conference on the Changes to the Cabinet and Other Appointments on 22 July.

“Energy is becoming increasingly central to Singapore’s economic competitiveness, industrial transformation and long-term resilience,” Wong said.

Tan See Leng will be appointed Minister for Trade and Industry overseeing energy and industry from 27 July, whilst Deputy Prime Minister Gan Kim Yong will continue to oversee trade and Singapore’s broader economic strategy. 

Tan, who has been serving as Minister-in-charge of Energy and Science and Technology, will relinquish his position as Minister for Manpower.

In a separate statement, the MTI said the renaming reflects the government’s growing focus on energy. It will focus on securing stable and competitively priced energy whilst supporting Singapore’s transition towards its 2050 net-zero target.

The ministry’s existing energy initiatives include constructing a second liquefied natural gas terminal and centralising gas procurement for the power sector.

It is also expanding solar-energy deployment, seeking partnerships to import low-carbon electricity, and assessing technologies including nuclear energy.

data.title: UOB upgrades 300,000 Visa cards to new premium tiers across ASEAN
data.description:
Eligible cardholders will learn of their new card tiers beginning September 2026.

United Overseas Bank (UOB) has partnered with Visa to relaunch several of its card products under Visa’s newly introduced premium card tiers, Visa Infinite Privilege and Visa Infinite Private.

Over 300,000 cards across five markets—Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, Indonesia, and Vietnam—will be upgraded to higher card tiers, UOB said in a statement. Eligible UOB Visa Infinite cardholders will be notified of their new card tiers from September 2026 onwards.

All other cardholders in the five markets will continue to enjoy their existing privileges with no downgrades across the portfolio, UOB said.

Visa refreshed its Infinite offering on 16 July, with the two new card tiers. 

UOB is Visa’s largest card issuer in ASEAN based on total billings, the bank said, with over 8.5 million customers across the region.

The number of new UOB affluent cardholders reportedly grew over 10% year-on-year in 2025, whilst card billings for this segment surged more than 25% in the same year.

data.title: CAAS commits $4b to air navigation upgrades ahead of Changi T5 opening
data.description:
AI-powered tools will help controllers predict traffic flows and manage disruptions.

The Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore (CAAS) will invest $4b over the next 15 years to upgrade Singapore’s air navigation services as air travel demand rises.

The “man-machine-method” upgrade will focus on expanding the air traffic controller workforce, modernising air navigation systems and using technology such as artificial intelligence (AI) to improve air traffic management.

The investment comes as the International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) expects global air passenger volumes to nearly triple over the next 25 years, with Asia-Pacific accounting for half of the growth.

CAAS said air navigation providers must expand capacity and capabilities alongside airport infrastructure investments to ensure safe and efficient air travel.

Singapore currently manages one of the world’s busiest and most complex airspaces, with CAAS air traffic control officers (ATCOs) handling about 2,000 flights daily, including 1,000 flights arriving and departing Changi Airport.

The agency plans to increase its ATCO workforce by about 40% from 500 today to around 700 by the mid-2030s, ahead of the opening of Changi Airport Terminal 5 (T5).

To attract more talent, CAAS has introduced a S$20,000 sign-on bonus for new ATCO recruits from 1 June 2026. It will also develop an undergraduate degree specialisation in air traffic control with a local university, introduce an ATC Undergraduate Scholarship, and launch an ATC Management Associate Programme in August 2026.

Beyond manpower, CAAS will strengthen its engineering and research capabilities through the establishment of an Engineering Development Centre to develop and integrate new air navigation technologies.

It will also provide additional support for PhD students researching air traffic management through the Nanyang Technological University Research Scholarship scheme.

CAAS will upgrade more than 30 air navigation systems, including the NexGen Air Traffic Management System, which will replace the current system and double capacity.

The new system will support up to one million aircraft movements annually, process up to 4,000 aircraft positions in real time and increase controller workstations at the Singapore Air Traffic Control Centre from 100 to 150.

The system will also expand ATCO workstations at the Singapore Air Traffic Control Centre (SATCC) from 100 to 150, with completion expected by 2030.

CAAS will also introduce the Integrated Digital Tower System (IDTS), which will use cameras and sensors to provide controllers with a real-time digital view of Changi Airport’s aerodrome.

The system will increase aerodrome ATCO workstations from 20 to 54 and allow more flexible operating arrangements beyond physical control towers. Work will begin in August 2026, with initial operations expected in 2030 and full completion by the mid-2030s.

Other technology upgrades include the Info-Centric Air Traffic Management system, which will use data analytics and predictive tools to manage traffic flows, and the Open Platform for Air Navigation Services, which will enable digital information sharing with airlines, airports and regional air navigation providers.

Changi infrastructure upgrades underway

Additionally, CAAS will also refurbish and upgrade the SATCC and Changi Control Tower to support new systems and future three-runway operations at Changi Airport.

Upgrades to the SATCC will be completed in phases, with the next phase expected by end-2028. Works at the Changi Control Tower will begin in November 2026 and finish by end-2028.

The works will include façade improvements and the construction of a new annex building between the tower and Jewel Changi Airport.

The upgrades will support Changi Airport’s move towards three-runway operations by the end of the decade, which will increase flight handling capacity.

CAAS will also introduce AI-powered tools to support controllers, including a Multi Sector Planner, Digital ATCO Assistant and Automated Read-back Error Detection system.

From 2027, it will trial an AI-powered Multi Sector Planner that allows one planner to support two to three control sectors, reducing coordination workload.

CAAS will also introduce AI-powered tools to support controllers, including a Multi Sector Planner, Digital ATCO Assistant and Automated Read-back Error Detection system.

The agency said it is also working with regional aviation authorities to improve air traffic coordination, including daily operational calls with counterparts from China, Hong Kong, South Korea, Thailand and Vietnam.

“As a global air hub, we are committed to serving all airspace users and to deliver for them an even smoother, more seamless and safer travel experience,” said CAAS Director-General Han Kok Juan said. ”We plan ahead. We invest, partner and leverage technology to ensure that we have the capacity and capabilities we need to meet rising demand and benefit from aviation’s growth.”

data.title: MLT to sell China logistics assets, Singapore warehouse
data.description:
The Wuxi properties have a combined agreed value of $137.9m. 

Mapletree Logistics Trust (MLT) plans to divest two logistics properties in China with a combined agreed property value of $137.9m (RMB724m), as well as a warehouse in Singapore.

The trust’s two wholly owned subsidiaries have entered into conditional sale and purchase agreements to divest their 100% equity interests in the companies that own the properties, according to a bourse filing.

The assets—Mapletree (Wuxi) Logistics Park (MWLP1) and Mapletree Wuxi New District Logistics Park (MWLP2)—will be sold to a new renminbi fund for $34.3m (RMB180m) and $103.6m (RMB544m), respectively.

The cash consideration will be based on the adjusted net asset values of the companies, taking into account the agreed property values and intercompany loans. 

The transaction is classified as an interested-party transaction because the fund is an associate of Mapletree Investments, which is a controlling unitholder of MLT.

MWLP1 comprises three blocks of single-storey warehouses with a net lettable area of 45,084 square metres (sq. m.) on a land site of about 67,800 sq. m.

Independent valuers placed its value at between $32m (RMB168m) and $32.2m (RMB169m).

Meanwhile, MWLP2 consists of four blocks of two-storey ramp-up warehouses with a net lettable area of 122,403 sq. m. on a land area of nearly 100,000 sq. m.

It was valued at between $102.1m (RMB536m) and $103m (RMB541m).

MLT expects to receive the proceeds from the China divestments within six months of signing the sale agreements.

In a separate filing, the trust announced plans to sell 39 Changi South Avenue 2 in Singapore to CS Ceramiche for $16.588m.

The price is 20% above the warehouse’s latest valuation of $13.8m as at March 31, 2026.

The three-storey cargo-lift warehouse has a net lettable area of about 6,129 sq. m. and was completed in 1997.

The transaction is subject to conditions including shareholder approval from CS Ceramiche’s parent, Indigo Star Holdings, and compliance with conditions imposed by JTC Corporation.

It is expected to be completed by the third quarter of FY2026/27.

data.title: Universal Robina Corporation expands snack portfolio in Singapore
data.description:
DKSH Business Unit Consumer Goods will support the distribution and market expansion.

Universal Robina Corporation (URC) has extended its partnership with DKSH Business Unit Consumer Goods to strengthen the distribution and market presence of its snack and beverage brands in Singapore, including Jack ’n Jill Potato Chips, Roller Coaster chips, and C2 ready-to-drink tea beverages.

Under the extended collaboration, DKSH will continue providing market expansion services for URC across Singapore, covering sales, distribution, merchandising, field activation, and route-to-market execution across modern trade, general trade, convenience stores, and e-commerce channels.

The partnership builds on several years of collaboration between the two companies, including DKSH’s distribution support for Munchy’s, one of Singapore’s leading biscuit brands with a presence across key retail channels.

URC said the extension reflects the results achieved through its collaboration with DKSH and its confidence in the company’s ability to continue supporting the growth of its Jack ’n Jill snack portfolio.

The partnership will also support the introduction and growth of C2 ready-to-drink tea beverages in Singapore, broadening URC’s consumer goods portfolio in the market.

data.title: CCS reviews Analogic-Leidos merger over airport security market overlap
data.description:
The merger overlaps in the supply of checked and cabin baggage explosives detection systems

The Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCS) is inviting public feedback on the proposed merger of the Analogic Corporation and Leidos Inc’s security enterprise solution as well as industrial and general automation businesses.

The proposed merger will overlap in the global supply of checked baggage explosives detection systems (EDS), and cabin baggage EDS, including after-market services, to Singapore.

Analogic produces imaging and detection solutions in the healthcare, industrial, and aviation security sectors globally and in Singapore. In the aviation security sector, Analogic is active in cabin baggage EDS and checked baggage EDS.

Analogic is controlled by affiliates of Altaris, LLC, a New York-based investment firm, the CCS said.

Leidos is a science and technology solutions company headquartered in Virginia, United States. Leidos provides scientific engineering, systems integration and technical services, with a primary focus on the national security, defence and health sectors, the CCS said.

CCS will accept public feedback on the proposed merger until 5 August 2026. Public feedback can be shared through the online form or an email at [email protected], the commission said.

data.title: OUE REIT NPI rises 4.8% to $110.3m in H1
data.description:
Revenue increased 3.8% to $136.1m during the period.

OUE REIT's net property income (NPI) rose 4.8% year-on-year (YoY) to $110.3m in the first half (H1) of 2026, according to a bourse filing.

Revenue for the six months ended 30 June grew 3.8% to $136.1m. Finance costs fell 16.6% to $37.8m from $45.3m.

Distribution per unit increased 28.6% to 1.26 Singapore cents from 0.98 cents, whilst the amount available for distribution went up by 28.6% to $69.8m from $54.3m.

The REIT will pay its H1 2026 distribution on 3 September, with books closing on 30 July.

It recorded $8.2m in share of results from its 50% stake in OUE Bayfront, up 30.6% YoY. 

Meanwhile, its newly acquired 19.9% stake in Sydney's Salesforce Tower, completed on 16 March, contributed $2.2m during the period.

OUE REIT said first-half hospitality performance strengthened, whilst contributions from its Singapore commercial portfolio remained stable. 

Hospitality revenue rose 11.2% to $50.1m, whilst the segment’s NPI increased 12.3% to $45.1m. 

The commercial segment saw revenue slip 0.1% to $86m, whilst NPI edged up 0.1% to $65.3m.

data.title: CSA strengthens cyber rules for critical infrastructure and cloud
data.description:
New cloud security code will launch in 2H2026.

The Cyber Security Agency of Singapore (CSA) will release an updated Cybersecurity Code of Practice (CCoP) for Critical Information Infrastructure (CII) and a new CCoP for Cloud Services later this year.

The announcement was made by Minister for Digital Development and Information Josephine Teo at the Operational Technology Cybersecurity Expert Panel Forum 2026.

The revised CII code is the first update since 2022 and comes amid the rise of AI-enabled cyber threats. According to CSA, frontier AI is enabling threat actors to identify vulnerabilities and launch attacks more quickly, shortening the window for organisations to respond.

To address these risks, the updated code will include new technical guidance on adversarial attack simulation, penetration testing and threat hunting.

“The updates to CCoP focus on strengthening CII governance, visibility, detection and readiness, and broader enterprise networks that are interconnected with the CIIs to align with the amendments made to the Cybersecurity Act,” the agency said.

Under the updated code, organisations will be required to strengthen board and senior management accountability by maintaining an annually reviewed cyber resilience framework covering risk tolerance, mitigation, transfer and recovery.

CII owners must also obtain Cyber Trust Mark Level 5 certification, maintain oversight of interconnected systems linked to critical infrastructure, participate in CSA-led deployment of threat detection systems across network segments, develop comprehensive cybersecurity exercise plans, and implement stronger network management, monitoring and detection measures.

Alongside the revised CII code, CSA will launch a new CCoP for Cloud Services in the second half of 2026 to establish cybersecurity requirements for the secure deployment, operation and management of CII systems hosted on cloud platforms.

The agency said the new code reflects the increasing adoption of cloud technologies by critical infrastructure operators and aims to ensure cloud environments remain protected against evolving cyber threats.

CSA developed the framework following consultations with auditors and CII owners that are using or considering cloud services. Feedback from these engagements was incorporated into the final controls and implementation guidance.

To support adoption, CSA has partnered with Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud and Microsoft Azure to develop cloud provider-specific Companion Guides.

data.title: Category A car COE premiums fall to $126k in July
data.description:
It recorded 1,590 bids for 1,245 available certificates.

Category A certificate of entitlement (COE) premiums fell in Singapore’s second July bidding exercise, with small-car COEs closing at $126,000, the Land Transport Authority (LTA) said.

The category recorded 1,237 successful bids out of 1,590 received, leaving eight certificates unused from the quota of 1,245. Its previous quota premium (PQP) stood at $125,646.

Category B, which covers larger and more powerful cars, recorded the highest premium amongst the vehicle categories at $129,890, above its PQP of $127,835. The category received 1,365 bids, of which 856 were successful, leaving 15 unused certificates from a quota of 871.

For commercial vehicles, Category C premiums settled at $93,889, with all 301 available certificates allocated after receiving 430 bids. The category's PQP stood at $92,599.

Motorcycle COEs under Category D eased to $10,202, compared with a PQP of $9,923. All 562 available certificates were taken up from 620 bids received.

The Open Category (Category E), which can be used for any vehicle type except motorcycles but is typically used for larger cars, closed at $129,971. The category attracted 450 bids, with all 263 available certificates allocated.

LTA said the quota for the exercise included unallocated COEs carried over from the second bidding exercise in June 2026.

Category A attracted 1,590 bids for a quota of 1,245 COEs, with 1,237 successful bids. Category B received 1,365 bids for 871 COEs, resulting in 856 successful bids.

Category C recorded 430 bids for 301 COEs, all of which were allocated. Category D received 620 bids for 562 COEs, with all certificates allocated. Category E attracted 450 bids for 263 COEs, all of which were allocated.

The second July COE bidding exercise ended on 22 July at 4 p.m.
